items.title: How H.H. Holmes Became America’s First Serial Killer
items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>Long before he was hanged in 1896 for murdering his business partner, Herman Webster Mudgett, better known as Dr. Henry Howard Holmes, had built a reputation for fraud, deception, and violence. He also built an infamous "Murder Castle" in Chicago, a labyrinthine building allegedly designed to lure and kill travelers attending the 1893 World's Fair. Historians have confirmed at least nine victims, but many believe the true death toll may have been far, far higher.

On this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift we separate the historical record from the sensational rumors surrounding H. H. Holmes, exploring the crimes, scams, and enduring mysteries that turned him into one of history's most notorious figures.

Do you believe the legends about H.H. Holmes and his"Murder Castle"? Let us know in the comments!


Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews

#</article>

items.title: The Miserable End Of Henry VIII | Last Days
items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>Ravaged by illness, consumed by paranoia, and fully aware that his reign was coming to an end, King Henry VIII spent his last days doing everything he could to protect his son Edward's future ascent to the throne. But as they often did with Henry... things got bloody and people got executed.

LAST DAYS is Weird History’s prestige format series focusing on final chapters, deaths, downfalls and conclusions. In this premiere episode, we examine the shocking true story behind Henry VIII's last execution, the brutal politics of the Tudor dynasty, and the dark final days of one of history's most notorious monarchs.

Do you think Henry was paranoid? Or was he right to be worried about Edward's reign? Let un know in the comments.

(Much like real history, this video was produced entirely by human beings.)

Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews

#noai #henryviii #weirdhistory #royalfamily  #royalhistory  #englishhistory</article>

items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>From world leaders, to rock n roll bands, to television shows, sports teams, amusement parks, scientific eras, religious movements, and even nations… eventually, everything ends.

Last Days is Weird History’s new prestige series focusing on final chapters, deaths, downfalls and conclusions. It details what happened and why, how it was important to world history, and what it the events may still have to teach us.

items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>During the middle ages roles in societies were very defined... Royalty was royalty and commoners were the commoners. However, sometimes they DID interact! Also, in those days, barbers did a lot more than just cut hair.. These aspects of medieval life, combined with diving into the typical Medieval Diet, and many other topics, are on deck today. items.title: The Dark Life of Aleister Crowley
items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>From founding a new spiritual philosophy to influencing modern occultism, yoga, and pop culture, Aleister Crowley built a legacy that still sparks fascination and debate more than a century later.

In this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift, we attempt to uncover the truth behind Crowley's dark reputation as "the wickedest man in the world", his bizarre rituals, infamous scandals, and the beliefs that made him one of the most mysterious figures of the 20th century. Was he an evil mastermind, a misunderstood visionary, or something in between?

What's the craziest story you ever heard about Aleister Crowley? Let us know in the comments.

Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews

#aleistercrowley #occult #weirdhistory</article>

items.title: We’ve Been Wrong About the Trojan Horse
items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>The legendary tale of the Trojan War has captivated people for thousands of years, filled with Greek gods, epic battles, mysterious prophecies, and the famous giant wooden horse said to have brought down the city of Troy. But was the Trojan Horse real, or is it one of history's greatest myths?

On this episode of Weird History, we examine the possibility that historians have misunderstood the Trojan Horse all along. Was it truly a massive wooden horse hiding Greek soldiers, or could it have been something entirely different?

Do you think there was really a Trojan Horse? Let us know in the comments.

(Much like real history, this video was produced entirely by human beings.) 


Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews



#trojanhorse #greece #weirdhistory</article>

items.title: Secrets of the Winchester Mystery House
items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>The Winchester Mystery House is one of America's most famous haunted mansions. Built by Sarah Winchester, heiress to the Winchester Rifle fortune, this bizarre mansion is filled with secret passageways, staircases that lead nowhere, and rooms designed to confuse both visitors and, according to legend, restless spirits.

In this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift, we explore the chilling legends and fascinating history behind the mysterious Winchester House. Was it built to escape a deadly curse? Is it truly haunted, or is there a more rational explanation behind its strange architecture? 

What's your take on the ghosts of Winchester Mystery House? Let us know in the comments.

Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews

#hauntedmansion #paranormal #weirdhistory</article>

items.title: Why This Extremely Expensive Doll Is So Controversial
items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>When you think of an American Girl doll, you probably think of wholesome history lessons, inspiring role models, and childhood nostalgia.

But despite their educational mission, American Girl has found itself at the center of controversy again and again. From surprising historical inaccuracies to culture wars and collector backlash, the beloved doll brand has repeatedly sparked debates no toy company ever expected.

On this episode of Weird History we dive into the doll’s history and try and figure out why the  beloved line can’t stay out of trouble.

(Much like real history, this video was produced entirely by human beings.) 

Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews

#noai #americangirl #weirdhistory</article>

items.title: The Real Life Dexter Copycat Killer
items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>The disturbing phenomenon of copycat killers has blurred the line between entertainment and reality for decades. In one of the most chilling true crime cases, Mark Andrew Twitchell, an aspiring serial killer from Canada, became obsessed with the hit TV series Dexter, modeling his crimes after television's infamous vigilante.

On this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift, we take a look at the bizarre and horrifying story of how a TV antihero influenced a real-life killer, the shocking evidence investigators uncovered, and why the case became one of the most infamous examples of life imitating art.

Are you a fan of Dexter? Let us know in the comments.


Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews

#dexter #truecrimestories #weirdhistory</article>

items.title: Why The German Army Feared The Night Witches
items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>On the dark battlefields of World War II, German soldiers feared an enemy they could barely see and rarely heard... until it was too late. Out of the blackness came a faint whooshing sound. Seconds later, bombs rained from the sky, buildings crumbled, and chaos erupted across the Eastern Front. On this episode of Weird History we take a look at the mysterious Night Witches: an elite squadron of all-female Soviet pilots who became one of the most feared forces of World War II. Flying outdated wooden biplanes on daring nighttime bombing missions, they terrorized German troops, earned countless military honors, and cemented their place among history's most legendary warriors. Have you ever heard of the Night Witches? Let us know in the comments. 


(Much like real history, this video was produced entirely by human beings.) 



Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews

#wwii #nightwitches #weirdhistory</article>

items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>The History of Medicine is a wild one. How humans figured out cures and even developed surgical procedures that saved lives! Often times what seemed to work in the past was later found to be detrimental to health and then abandoned. Today on Weird History we go into various aspects of the history of Medicine, the good, the bad, and the out-right miraculous!
What is your favorite part about the history of all things medical? Let us know in the comments!

items.title: The Ghosts Of Alcatraz
items.author: Weird History
items.content:
<article>Alcatraz, also known as "The Rock," was a maximum-security fortress where only the most dangerous and clever criminals were sent. One of the most infamous prisons in American history, it sat alone on a rocky island in San Francisco Bay and was once considered escape-proof. But decades after the prison closed, questions still linger. Did some prisoners actually escape? What secrets remain hidden behind its crumbling walls? And could Alcatraz be a place where even death isn't enough to set you free?

In this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift we take a look at the strange history of Alcatraz, the prison that may never let go. 

Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews

#haunted #alcatraz #weirdhistory</article>
